title: Fix beszel agent Ansible role for Arch Linux
description: The beszel_agent role only handles Debian and Alpine. Omarchy is Arch-based, so the systemd unit file creation and service start tasks are skipped. Add Arch/pacman support to the role.
close_reason: Omarchy abandoned - LUKS unlock + no GPU acceleration makes headless Wayland VM unworkable

title: Evaluate virtio-gl display when iGPU is available
description: QXL is used because the Intel Iris Xe iGPU is passed through to dockerhost (VMID 100). If iGPU passthrough is ever removed, switch to virtio-gl for better 3D acceleration. Requires libgl1, libegl1, and /dev/dri/renderD* on host.
close_reason: Omarchy abandoned - LUKS unlock + no GPU acceleration makes headless Wayland VM unworkable

title: Set up remote desktop access for Omarchy
description: noVNC via Proxmox works but is clunky. Investigate better options: xrdp (may not work well with Sway/Wayland), or accept SSH+tmux as primary workflow. virt-viewer not available on macOS Homebrew.
close_reason: wayvnc installed via Ansible, binds 0.0.0.0:5900, auto-starts with Hyprland

title: Omarchy desktop VM on Proxmox
description: Set up Omarchy (Arch-based Sway desktop) as a VM on nuc13 Proxmox. OpenTofu for provisioning, Ansible for configuration. VMID 103, 4 cores, 8GB RAM, 50GB disk, QXL/SPICE display. Tailscale connected.
close_reason: Omarchy abandoned - LUKS unlock + no GPU acceleration makes headless Wayland VM unworkable

title: Pass through Intel Iris Xe iGPU to Ollama LXC
description: NUC13 has Intel Iris Xe Graphics (Raptor Lake-P, i7-1360P) but /dev/dri is empty — i915 driver not loaded on Proxmox host. Steps: 1) Load i915 module on Proxmox (may need to remove from blacklist). 2) Verify /dev/dri/renderD128 appears. 3) Pass /dev/dri into Ollama LXC (VMID 201) via lxc.mount.entry or cgroup device allow. 4) Install Intel compute runtime (intel-opencl-icd or oneAPI) inside the LXC. 5) Configure Ollama to use GPU. Note: Iris Xe has only 96 EUs so gains may be modest vs CPU.

title: Add AdGuard DNS entry for omarchy
description: Add omarchy.home.alborworld.com Traefik route proxying to Sunshine web UI (https://omarchy.home:47990). AdGuard DNS entry for omarchy.home already exists.
close_reason: Omarchy abandoned - LUKS unlock + no GPU acceleration makes headless Wayland VM unworkable

title: Make beszel_agent role OS-agnostic (Alpine + Ubuntu)
description: The beszel_agent role currently uses apk and OpenRC (Alpine-only). Add conditional logic to support apt and systemd for Ubuntu hosts. Needed for Ollama and OpenClaw LXC monitoring.
close_reason: Role now supports Alpine (OpenRC) and Debian/Ubuntu (systemd). Deployed to both Ollama and OpenClaw LXCs.

title: Enable remote LUKS unlock for omarchy VM
description: Omarchy VM has full disk encryption (LUKS) which requires entering the passphrase via Proxmox console on every reboot. Investigate options: Dropbear in initramfs for SSH unlock, TPM auto-unlock, or removing encryption. This blocks unattended reboots and onboot autostart.
close_reason: Omarchy abandoned - LUKS unlock + no GPU acceleration makes headless Wayland VM unworkable

title: OpenClaw on Proxmox
description: Deploy OpenClaw AI assistant and Ollama inference server as separate LXC containers on Proxmox (nuc13). Two-stage IaC: OpenTofu for container creation + Ansible for provisioning. Ollama LXC (VMID 201): 4 cores, 12GB RAM, 30GB disk, serves llama3.1 8B — DEPLOYED AND WORKING. OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202): 2 cores, 4GB RAM, 20GB disk — NEEDS INSTALLER VERIFICATION AND MANUAL ONBOARDING. Open WebUI on dockerhost — DEPLOYED AND WORKING at chat.home.alborworld.com. Traefik dynamic configs for ollama.home.alborworld.com and openclaw.home.alborworld.com in place. AdGuard DNS rewrites configured (ollama.home→10.0.4.123, openclaw.home→10.0.4.66). Key learnings: TraefikKop requires host port = container port for loadbalancer.server.port label. LXCs need TUN device for Tailscale (added via pct set and Tofu null_resource).

title: Scaffold OpenTofu stack for OpenClaw LXC
description: Create tofu/proxmox/openclaw/ with main.tf, provider.tf, backend.tf, variables.tf, .env.template. Ubuntu 22.04 template, unprivileged container, 2 cores, 4GB RAM, 20GB disk. SSH bootstrap (no TUN needed). Reuse provider pattern from tailscale-exit-nordvpn-nl.
close_reason: Closed

title: Update architecture docs with OpenClaw/Ollama/Open WebUI
description: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md has no mention of the new infrastructure deployed in the OpenClaw epic. Document: OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202), Ollama LXC (VMID 201), Open WebUI on dockerhost, Traefik dynamic configs, AdGuard DNS rewrites, Tailscale hostnames, and the security architecture (iptables, service isolation, SOPS secrets).
close_reason: Updated all 5 sections: Tailscale nodes table, new AI Services section, Service Orchestration, Security Practices, Backups, and Automation & CI/CD

title: Add dedicated VLAN for OpenClaw LXC
description: OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202) sits on the default vmbr0 bridge with no VLAN isolation. Per merox.dev deployment guide, it should have a dedicated VLAN in Proxmox for network segmentation. However, the existing iptables rules (default DROP) + Tailscale mesh already provide strong isolation. The practical security gain of adding a VLAN is minimal for a homelab — revisit if untrusted devices join the LAN or if the threat model changes. Would require: new VLAN on router, Proxmox bridge VLAN tag in Tofu, static IP on new subnet, firewall rule updates.
notes: 2026-02-23: Assessed as low priority. Existing iptables (default DROP) + Tailscale provide equivalent isolation. VLAN adds marginal security for a homelab but introduces router/provisioning complexity. Static DHCP reservation set on router for LXC IP. Deprioritized to P4 — revisit if untrusted devices join the LAN or for learning exercise.

title: Add log monitoring and alerting for OpenClaw
description: No automated monitoring of OpenClaw gateway logs for suspicious patterns (unauthorized access, injection attempts, error spikes). Set up log forwarding and alerts — could use Beszel, a simple logwatch cron, or Promtail/Loki if available.
notes: Considering Promtail+Loki+Grafana but no existing stack yet. Deferred until logging infra is in place.

title: Pin OpenClaw version and add CVE verification
description: OpenClaw is installed via npm without version pinning — always pulls latest. Pin to a specific version in the Ansible role and add a verification step to check against known CVEs (e.g., CVE-2025-6514 for mcp-remote). Update ansible/roles/openclaw/ defaults and tasks.

title: Add audio transcription with Whisper to OpenClaw
description:
Add voice message transcription using faster-whisper so OpenClaw can process audio/ogg messages.

Steps:
1. Install faster-whisper runtime in a Python venv on the OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202), plus ffmpeg
2. Create ~/bin/transcribe_ogg.py script using WhisperModel('base', device='cpu', compute_type='int8')
3. Hook into OpenClaw inbound message flow: detect audio/ogg media, run transcription, replace message text
4. Quality tuning: vad_filter=True, consider model size (base→small→medium), set language for speed
5. Optional: enable TTS responses for full voice loop

Key details:
- Use faster-whisper (CPU, int8) as the runtime — easiest for LXC without GPU
- Script takes an audio file path, outputs transcribed text to stdout
- Integration point is the inbound message handler/middleware — add conditional for audio/* mimeType
- Model 'base' for speed, upgrade to 'small'/'medium' for accuracy later
close_reason: Added Ansible IaC for Whisper transcription: faster-whisper venv, transcribe_ogg.py template, wrapper script, systemd PATH update, architecture docs updated

title: Fix systemctl --user bus error in OpenClaw LXC
description: OpenClaw gateway fails to start with: 'Gateway service check failed: Error: systemctl --user unavailable: Failed to connect to bus: No medium found'. This is because the openclaw service runs as a system service (not user), but OpenClaw internally tries to use systemctl --user which requires a D-Bus user session. Need to either enable lingering for the openclaw user (loginctl enable-linger) or configure XDG_RUNTIME_DIR so the user bus is available inside the LXC.
close_reason: Fixed by adding XDG_RUNTIME_DIR and DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS to systemd service template (resolved via Ansible getent), plus loginctl enable-linger

title: Change TTS provider to ElevenLabs
description: Switch OpenClaw's TTS provider from current (likely OpenAI) to ElevenLabs Free. Steps: 1) Get ElevenLabs API key, add to SOPS secrets. 2) Choose a voice in ElevenLabs → Voices, copy Voice ID. 3) Update OpenClaw TTS config (openclaw.json or Ansible-managed config) with ElevenLabs provider, API key, and voice ID. 4) Optionally create voice presets (e.g. calm, energetic). 5) Restart OpenClaw and test.
close_reason: ElevenLabs TTS configured via Ansible with multilingual v2 model and voice ID 8JVbfL6oEdmuxKn5DK2C

title: Investigate Proxmox firewall rules for OpenClaw LXC via Tofu
description: Check if the bpg/proxmox Tofu provider supports defining firewall rules at the container level. If so, codify rules equivalent to the current iptables setup (default DROP inbound except from Proxmox host, restrict outbound to 80/443) in the OpenClaw Tofu stack. Proxmox-level firewall is stronger than in-container iptables since it can't be bypassed if the container is compromised.
notes: bpg/proxmox provider (v0.70+) fully supports container-level firewall via two resources: proxmox_virtual_environment_firewall_options (default policy DROP/ACCEPT per container) and proxmox_virtual_environment_firewall_rules (specific inbound/outbound rules with ports, protocols, source IPs). Rules enforced at hypervisor level — can't be bypassed from inside LXC. No firewall config exists in the stack yet. Implementation: add both resources to tofu/proxmox/openclaw/main.tf targeting container_id=202 on nuc13. Need to account for Tailscale UDP, DNS, and Ollama connectivity.
close_reason: Proxmox hypervisor-level firewall applied via Tofu. Default DROP inbound/outbound with allowlists for SSH (LAN), Tailscale WireGuard/STUN, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS. Verified Tailscale connectivity post-apply.

title: Verify and enable Proxmox cluster-level firewall
description: The Proxmox cluster firewall (cluster.fw) has no enable option set, meaning per-container firewall rules in 202.fw may not be enforced. Need to: 1) Test if container-level rules work without cluster firewall enabled 2) If not, enable cluster-level firewall via pvesh/API 3) Consider managing this in Tofu. Risk: enabling cluster firewall could affect all VMs/LXCs on nuc13 if they don't have their own firewall configs.
close_reason: Verified: Proxmox per-container firewall (202.fw enable:1) IS enforced without cluster-level firewall. Test: flushed in-container iptables, port 853 still blocked by Proxmox layer, port 80 still allowed. Cluster-level firewall is optional — per-container enable flag is sufficient. No action needed.

title: Remove unnecessary postfix from OpenClaw LXC
description: Postfix is listening on 127.0.0.1:25 and [::1]:25 inside the OpenClaw LXC. Likely installed by default with Ubuntu 24.04. Not needed for OpenClaw operation — unnecessary attack surface. Remove the package or disable the service.

title: Create Ansible role for OpenClaw provisioning

close_reason: Closed

title: Bind beszel-agent to Tailscale interface only in OpenClaw LXC
description: beszel-agent listens on *:45876 (all interfaces including eth0/LAN). Both iptables and Proxmox firewall block inbound access, but it should be bound to the Tailscale IP (100.73.237.11) or localhost only for defense-in-depth. Check beszel-agent config options or Ansible role for listen address setting.

Verify OpenClaw connects to Ollama and responds. Verify Tailscale-only access to both dashboards. Document any manual steps (e.g. Tailscale approval, model pull).
notes: SESSION 2026-02-22 FINAL STATE: OpenClaw v2026.2.21-2 deployed on VMID 202. Gateway running as user systemd service (openclaw-gateway). Telegram bot configured (token in openclaw.json). Model switched from Ollama local to cloud provider via 'openclaw configure --section model'. Pairing approved for Telegram user 104769734 (code NYRDPXVN).
